distribution_hierarchy.location: external genset enclosure + ATS in ground-floor plant room

selectivity_cascade._note: F01 800A Type D upstream / 250A downstream main = 3.2:1 ratio, above IEC 60898 typical 3:1 selectivity threshold.

F02 800A Type D upstream / 400A downstream main = 2.0:1 ratio, BELOW the IEC 60898 typical 3:1 selectivity threshold. Manufacturer selectivity-table consultation required for design intent; in practice the short-time grading on modern MCCBs typically maintains discrimination at this ratio, but cannot be claimed without the device-pair-specific table.
F03 800A Type D upstream / 250A downstream main = 3.2:1 ratio, above IEC 60898 typical threshold. Mechanical inrush (motor starts on M01/M03) handled by D-curve coordination.

F04 800A Type D upstream / 63A Type C downstream main = 12.7:1 ratio, deeply selective. CRITICAL: F04 fire_alarm feeder has NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 §560 — life-safety isolation maintained at MSB level; RCDs at MSB intake would trip the entire DB-FA1 panel on a single earth-fault and disable fire-alarm functionality.

F05 800A Type D upstream / 40A Type C downstream main = 20.0:1 ratio, deeply selective. F05 emergency-lighting feeder has NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 — central-battery / sustained-supply EM lighting requires same life-safety isolation pattern as fire-alarm. Earth-fault clearance via TN-C-S adiabatic 5s per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.2.2.

F06 800A Type D upstream / 32A Type C downstream main = 25.0:1 ratio, deeply selective. F06 comms feeder carries a Type B RCD upstream of DB-COMMS per IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3 (DC-leakage-tolerant for IT/SMPS loads — BS EN 50173:2018 EMC). Type B selectivity vs final-circuit Type A RCDs on data circuits is achieved by time-delay (S-type or selective Type B) at the feeder tier.

F07 800A Type D upstream / 50A Type C downstream main = 16.0:1 ratio, deeply selective. F07 feeds the UPS input (10 kVA online double-conversion); the UPS-output side of DB-UPS is electrically downstream of the UPS rectifier+inverter pair and therefore decoupled from the upstream MSB during normal operation. Bypass-mode selectivity (when UPS is in static-bypass) reverts to the F07 cascade ratio.

F08 800A Type D upstream / 63A Type D MCCB downstream main = 12.7:1 ratio, deeply selective. F08 feeds the utility-side input of the ATS changeover (utility-priority mode per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552). Genset-side input bypasses the MSB entirely — genset-mode selectivity is documented in the fault-level skill's full IR (Ik" is substantially lower on standby source; instantaneous trip settings on G02/G03/G04 may need re-verification during commissioning). D-curve on F08 + D-curve on G02/G03 handle motor-start inrush propagation.

spd_assessment.location_basis: commercial 4-floor office with moderate lightning risk and life-safety present (DB-FA1 fire-alarm + DB-EM emergency-lighting central battery + DB-UPS critical loads + DB-COMMS data + DB-GENSET-XCV standby + occupied tenancies). Type 2 SPD at MSB intake per IEC 60364-4-44 §443.4 — sufficient for indoor commercial without site-LPS (would escalate to Type 1+2 if IEC 62305 LPS installed).

assumptions: Diversity factor applied per sub-DB load class: lighting 0.95, small-power 0.85, mechanical 0.75, fire-alarm 1.0. Aggregate Imax_total = (19.3×0.95 + 47.5×0.85 + 50.0×0.75 + 3.0×1.0) kW ≈ 98.5 kW; Imax = 98,500 / (√3 × 400 × 0.85) ≈ 167A — rounded to 170A. Inline LLM derivation; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3 to calc.sld_system_metrics.

Sub-DB summed loads (19.3 + 47.5 + 50.0 + 3.0 = 119.8 kW total) are smaller than the MSB feeder rollups (F01=80kW + F02=150kW + F03=100kW + F04=12kW = 342kW). The feeder rollups in the MSB intent reflect rated headroom for spare-way / tenant fit-out; actual diversified Imax uses the sub-DB summed loads as the authoritative basis.

F02 cascade is 'partial_selective' (2:1 below 3:1 IEC 60898 typical) — documented in selectivity_cascade._note. NOT a compliance failure: the cascade verdict captures the engineering caveat, and the design relies on manufacturer selectivity tables (instantaneous + short-time grading on modern MCCB pairs typically holds at this ratio). If tighter discrimination is required, downstream main can be reduced to 315A (gives 2.5:1, still below 3:1) or upstream raised to 1250A.

DB-FA1 fire_alarm_panel dedicated supply confirmed: F04 has NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 §560 (life-safety isolation). Final-circuit fire-alarm devices (FA01-FA06) inside DB-FA1 are also rcd_protected: false. PE-via-MET earth-fault clearance time relies on TN-C-S adiabatic + 5-second clearance per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.2.2 (verified by db-layout/v1.2.0 panel-level checks).

Peak PFC 22.5kA at MSB busbar is sourced from the fault-level intent's utility-source worst-case transformer-secondary value (TX-1 ifault_ka_max = 22.5 kA per IEC 60909-0:2016 cascade) — supersedes prior LLM estimate of 15.5kA which inferred from declared 16kA service tail. Still comfortably inside the 50kA Icu rating of the 800A main switch (55% headroom).

Emergency lighting via local battery-pack luminaires per IEC 60364-5-56 §560.7 (not central inverter); no dedicated emergency-lighting feeder at MSB level — DB-L1 L06 is the only `voltage_class: emergency_lighting` circuit and serves as a local sustained-supply distribution to battery-pack luminaires.

Multi-skill intent consumption (v1.4): earthing intent at electrical/earthing/examples/intl-rural-tncs/intent-out.json (folder renamed from intl-rural-tt to intl-rural-tncs in Sprint A.1 C1 cause-fix — name was a lie; content has always been TN-C-S commercial) provides system_type=TN-C-S, ze_declared_ohm=0.30, supply_bond_type=utility_pen_bond — cross-checked against SLD supply_origin per INV-11.

Multi-skill intent consumption (v1.4): fault-level intent at electrical/fault-level/examples/intl-commercial-with-genset/intent-out.json models DUAL-SOURCE (utility 1600 kVA + 800 kVA standby genset, source_summary.type='mixed'). SLD consumes the worst-case utility-source PFC at transformer secondary; genset contribution is documented separately in the fault-level skill's full IR but NOT modeled in SLD's distribution_hierarchy. SLD's peak_pfc_ka reflects utility-source only — verify genset-mode protection settings via the fault-level skill's IR before commissioning (genset-only operation typically lowers Ik" and can fall below selectivity thresholds).
tool_call_pending_for_system_metrics: true — imax_total, SPD assessment, life-safety isolation remain LLM-estimated until calc.sld_system_metrics ships (WI3 deferral preserved).

Multi-skill cascade growth (v1.5): added 4 new sub-DBs as MSB-MAIN children via F05-F08 — DB-EM (emergency_lighting_panel, F05 40A MCB Type C, NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7), DB-COMMS (comms_data_panel, F06 32A MCB Type C, Type B RCD upstream per IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3), DB-UPS (ups_backed_critical_panel, F07 50A MCB Type C feeding 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS), DB-GENSET-XCV (ats_changeover_panel, F08 63A MCCB Type D feeding ATS utility-side input per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552). All four cascade pairs are fully_selective at ≥12.7:1 ratio — well above the 3:1 IEC 60898 typical threshold. Total board count 5 → 9; meta.consumed_intents 7 → 11 (4 new db-layout intents added at v1.3.0, ordered before earthing+fault-level per INV-11).

v1.5 cross-skill invariants preserved: supply_origin.system_type=TN-C-S still matches earthing intent (INV-11 check 6); system_metrics.peak_pfc_ka=22.5 still matches fault-level intent TX-1 transformer-secondary ifault_ka_max (INV-11 check 7) within ±0.5 kA tolerance. Adding sub-DBs further downstream does NOT change the peak PFC at the MSB busbar — it is a property of the utility supply, not the cascade structure. imax_total revised 170 → 190 A (+20 A engineer estimate for diversified contributions: DB-EM ~5 kW high-coincidence, DB-COMMS ~9 kW high-coincidence, DB-UPS ~11 kW typical, DB-GENSET-XCV ~24 kW mostly intermittent house loads during utility-priority normal mode — net diversified contribution ~12-14 kW = +18-21 A, midpoint 20 A).

Multi-sheet split (v1.5): drawing_layout.sheets = 2 because BOTH triggers fire — (a) hard count 9 boards > 8 threshold AND (b) functional isolation `fire_alarm_life_safety` (DB-FA1) coexists with `general_power` (DB-P1) and `lighting` (DB-L1) per BS 9999 §6.4 + IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 + NFPA 72 §10.6 (life-safety isolation mandates separate sheet from general-power groups). Sheet 1 carries MSB-MAIN + DB-L1 + DB-P1 + DB-M1 (main + general-power + mechanical group). Sheet 2 carries DB-FA1 + DB-EM + DB-UPS + DB-COMMS + DB-GENSET-XCV (life-safety + emergency-power + comms group). Both A1 ISO, ISO 19650:2018 + BS 1192:2007+A2:2016 generic INT, NTS scale.

F05-F08 feeders are asserted by the SLD's distribution_hierarchy_brief and are NOT yet present in the upstream MSB intent (`electrical/db-layout/examples/intl-commercial-tpn-msb/intent-out.json` carries F01-F04 only at v1.3.0). The cascade brief is the authoritative source at this growth-example tier; a v1.6 refresh of the MSB db-layout intent will reconcile by adding F05-F08 to the MSB schedule. The mismatch does NOT break INV-11 because consumed_intents records the per-board db-layout intents for DB-EM/DB-COMMS/DB-UPS/DB-GENSET-XCV, and each carries its own incoming_supply.fed_from documenting the feeder it expects.

drawing_notes: MSB-MAIN located in ground-floor plant room with 800A TPN intake from utility TN-C-S supply (Ze=0.30Ω, peak PFC 22.5kA from fault-level intent — utility-source worst case per IEC 60909-0:2016).

8 functionally-separated feeders from MSB: F01 lighting (250A) + F02 small-power (400A) + F03 mechanical (250A) + F04 fire-alarm (63A) + F05 emergency-lighting (40A) + F06 comms (32A) + F07 UPS-input (50A) + F08 ATS-utility (63A).
Type 2 SPD assembly at MSB intake per IEC 60364-4-44 §443.
Final-circuit RCDs (30mA Type A) located at sub-DBs DB-L1/DB-P1/DB-M1 — preserves MSB-level selectivity.
DB-FA1 fire-alarm panel: NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 §560. Earth-fault clearance by adiabatic 5s rule (IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.2.2).
F02 cascade (800A/400A = 2:1) flagged 'partial_selective' — confirm with manufacturer selectivity table before final design freeze.
DB-EM emergency-lighting central battery (BS EN 50171 3hr backup) — F05 has NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 (same life-safety isolation pattern as DB-FA1).
DB-UPS fed from 10 kVA online double-conversion UPS (F07 input side); UPS-output is electrically decoupled from MSB during normal operation. Bypass-mode selectivity reverts to F07 cascade ratio.

DB-GENSET-XCV ATS changeover (utility-priority per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552); standby genset 80 kVA bypasses the MSB on genset-mode — verify genset-source PFC + protection settings via the fault-level skill IR before commissioning.
DB-COMMS Type B RCD upstream per IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3 (DC-leakage-tolerant for IT/SMPS loads).

Multi-sheet drawing: 2 × A1 ISO sheets per v1.5 split (9 boards > 8 + life-safety isolation per BS 9999 §6.4 / IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560). Sheet 1: main + general-power + mechanical group. Sheet 2: life-safety + emergency-power + comms group.
flags: TOOL-CALL-PENDING:sld_system_metrics — System metrics are LLM-estimates; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.

rationale.chat_summary: Generic IEC commercial 4-floor office on TN-C-S 400V (Ze=0.30Ω). 800A MSB feeds 8 sub-DBs: lighting/power/mech/fire-alarm + EM-lighting/comms/UPS/ATS-genset. v1.5 multi-sheet (9 boards): Sheet 1 main+power+mech, Sheet 2 life-safety+emergency+comms per BS 9999 §6.4 / IEC §560. Peak PFC 22.5kA (fault-level TX-1, INV-11 preserved). 50kA Icu 55% headroom. F02 2:1 partial_selective; F05-F08 fully_selective. Type 2 SPD §443.

sections.title: Supply Origin
sections.summary: TN-C-S 400V TPN+E, declared Ze=0.30Ω + PFC=16kA. 800A MCCB main switch with 50kA Icu (IEC 60947-2). Generic IEC jurisdiction — citations use IEC 60364 form only.

decisions.label: TN-C-S supply confirmed
decisions.summary: PEN at service head, MET separation downstream of main switch.

sections.title: Distribution Hierarchy
sections.summary: 9-board cascade across 2 A1 sheets (v1.5). MSB-MAIN feeds 8 functionally-separated sub-DBs via dedicated feeders: F01 lighting (DB-L1), F02 small-power (DB-P1), F03 mechanical (DB-M1), F04 fire-alarm (DB-FA1), F05 emergency-lighting (DB-EM), F06 comms (DB-COMMS), F07 UPS-input (DB-UPS), F08 ATS-utility (DB-GENSET-XCV). Functional separation is the dominant design driver — life-safety (DB-FA1+DB-EM) gets isolated from general-power (DB-L1+DB-P1) per BS 9999 §6.4 / IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560, mechanical (DB-M1) kept separate to contain motor inrush, comms (DB-COMMS) on its own Type-B-RCD feeder for IT/SMPS DC-leakage tolerance, UPS (DB-UPS) decouples critical loads from utility, ATS (DB-GENSET-XCV) provides standby genset changeover. Sheet split per drawing_layout below.

decisions.label: Functional separation at MSB
decisions.summary: 8 feeders by voltage_class / load discipline rather than per-floor. Life-safety (DB-FA1, DB-EM) gets dedicated feeders with no upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560; mechanical (DB-M1) kept separate to avoid motor inrush propagating to lighting; comms (DB-COMMS) on Type-B-RCD feeder; UPS (DB-UPS) decouples critical loads; ATS (DB-GENSET-XCV) provides standby source per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-5-51 §514 functional separation; IEC 60364-5-56 §560+§552 life-safety + standby source

sections.title: System Metrics
sections.summary: Imax_total 190A (revised from v1.4 170A; +20A engineer estimate for 4 new sub-DBs diversified contribution — DB-EM/DB-COMMS/DB-UPS/DB-GENSET-XCV; vs 800A intake = 24% utilisation, 76% headroom). Peak PFC 22.5kA unchanged from v1.4 — sourced from fault-level intent TX-1 (utility-source worst case at transformer secondary per IEC 60909-0:2016 cascade); peak PFC is a property of the utility supply, not the cascade structure, so adding sub-DBs does NOT shift it. Still 55% Icu headroom (50kA breaker vs 22.5kA peak PFC). Genset-mode PFC is documented separately in the fault-level skill IR.

decisions.label: Intake capacity verified
decisions.summary: main_switch_rating 800A >> imax_total 190A; breaking_capacity 50kA >> peak_pfc 22.5kA (utility-source). 55% Icu headroom remains comfortable after v1.5 growth.

sections.title: Cascade Selectivity
sections.summary: 7 of 8 cascade pairs verified selective by IEC 60898 typical ratio. F02 (800A/400A = 2:1) flagged 'partial_selective' — below the 3:1 typical threshold but acceptable with manufacturer table consultation. F04 (12.7:1), F05 (20:1), F06 (25:1), F07 (16:1), F08 (12.7:1) are deeply selective — life-safety + emergency-power isolation preserved. F05+F08 cascade verification on standby/genset-mode operation requires re-check via fault-level skill IR before commissioning (Ik" lower on genset source).

decisions.label: F02 partial selectivity documented
decisions.summary: Verdict 'partial_selective' with note explaining ratio + remediation options (downsize child to 315A, upsize parent to 1250A, or accept with manufacturer selectivity-table confirmation).

decisions.label: F05-F08 fully selective at deep ratios
decisions.summary: All 4 new MSB→sub-DB cascade pairs added in v1.5 carry ratios ≥12.7:1 — well above the 3:1 IEC 60898 typical threshold. Sub-DB main ratings (40A, 32A, 50A, 63A) are tightly sized to actual diversified load, not feeder rating headroom, so the 800A upstream Type D gives generous margin.

sections.title: SPD Assessment
sections.summary: Type 2 SPD required at MSB intake per IEC 60364-4-44 §443. Commercial with moderate lightning risk + life-safety present — Type 2 baseline. Would escalate to Type 1+2 if site has IEC 62305 LPS.

decisions.label: Type 2 SPD at MSB
decisions.summary: IEC 60364-4-44 §443.4 baseline for indoor commercial without site-LPS.

sections.title: Life-Safety Isolation
sections.summary: Three life-safety / emergency-power panels established in v1.5: DB-FA1 (fire_alarm_panel, F04 NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 §560), DB-EM (emergency_lighting_panel, F05 NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 — central-battery BS EN 50171 3hr backup), DB-UPS (ups_backed_critical_panel, F07 feeds 10 kVA online UPS — 30-min ride-through critical IT loads). Earth-fault clearance on F04+F05 via TN-C-S adiabatic 5s. Multi-sheet split per BS 9999 §6.4 / IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 (life-safety isolated from general power on dedicated sheet). UPS-essential loads kVA = 10.

decisions.label: DB-FA1 + DB-EM dedicated supplies
decisions.summary: fire_alarm_dedicated_supply: true; emergency_lighting_dedicated_supply: true (v1.5 flip). NO upstream RCD on F04 (fire-alarm) or F05 (EM lighting). Final-circuit devices inside DB-FA1+DB-EM also rcd_protected: false. Earth-fault clearance via TN-C-S adiabatic 5s.

decisions.label: DB-UPS critical loads + DB-GENSET-XCV standby source
decisions.summary: ups_essential_loads_kva = 10 (10 kVA online double-conversion, 30-min ride-through per BS EN 62040 / IEC 62040). DB-GENSET-XCV ATS per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 (utility-priority) — 80 kVA standby genset bypasses MSB on genset-mode operation.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 standby source + BS EN 62040 UPS

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Compliant. No non-compliance flags. 13 assumptions documented covering diversity factor structure, sub-DB-summed-load basis, F02 partial selectivity rationale, DB-FA1 dedicated supply confirmation, peak PFC sourced from fault-level intent, emergency lighting strategy, earthing intent cross-check (INV-11), fault-level intent provenance with genset filtering note, WI3 tool deferral, v1.5 cascade growth (4 new sub-DBs), v1.5 cross-skill invariants preserved (peak_pfc_ka + system_type unchanged), v1.5 multi-sheet split rationale (BS 9999 §6.4 + IEC §560 life-safety isolation), and F05-F08 feeder-brief authoritative-source caveat.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: 13 assumptions captured in compliance_summary covering diversity factor structure (lighting 0.95 / power 0.85 / mechanical 0.75 / fire 1.0), authoritative basis for Imax derivation, F02 cascade caveat, DB-FA1 dedicated-supply confirmation, peak PFC sourced from fault-level intent (utility-source worst case, genset filtered), emergency lighting strategy, multi-skill earthing+fault-level INV-11 cross-checks, WI3 system-metrics tool deferral, v1.5 4-sub-DB growth (DB-EM/DB-COMMS/DB-UPS/DB-GENSET-XCV via F05-F08), v1.5 cross-skill INV-11 preservation, v1.5 multi-sheet split rationale, and F05-F08 brief-authoritative caveat.

sheets.split_rationale: Sheet 1 — General power + mechanical group, separated from life-safety per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560
Sheet 2 — Life-safety + comms + emergency-power group, isolated from general power per BS 9999 §6.4 / IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560

MSB-MAIN.layout_intent_note: Main switchboard at IEC TN-C-S intake — ground-floor plant room, root of distribution tree

DB-L1.layout_intent_note: Lighting distribution sub-DB — ground-floor lighting riser

DB-P1.layout_intent_note: General small-power sub-DB — ground-floor power riser

DB-FA1.layout_intent_note: Fire alarm panel — life-safety isolation per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 / BS 9999 §6.4; dedicated riser, not shared with general power

DB-EM.layout_intent_note: Emergency lighting central battery (BS EN 50171 3hr backup) — dedicated EM riser

DB-UPS.layout_intent_note: 10 kVA online UPS critical loads (30-min ride-through) — dedicated UPS-output riser

DB-COMMS.layout_intent_note: LV data/comms (Type B RCD per BS EN 50173) — dedicated comms riser, MDF

DB-GENSET-XCV.layout_intent_note: ATS standby genset changeover (IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552) — utility-priority mode
